user_query:
I’m an 80‑year‑old WWII veteran and hobby photographer. Please inspect the health of my computer using the local logs I’ve saved (don’t run live system commands). I want a clear, structured status summary focused on storage for my photos, plus an updated note I can read later.

Inputs (use all of these exactly as provided):
- input/system/disk_report.csv — columns: mount,size_gb,used_gb,available_gb
- input/system/cpu_mem_log.csv — columns: timestamp,cpu_percent,mem_used_mb
- input/system/pictures_inventory.csv — columns: file_name,folder,file_type,size_mb,date_taken (1 GB = 1024 MB for conversions)
- input/Photo-Backup-Notes.md — my existing notes; update the marked summary section only

What to produce:
1) Write a structured status file at output/system_summary.json with these top‑level keys and fields:
   - disk: array of { mount, size_gb, used_gb, avail_gb, used_pct } where used_pct = used_gb/size_gb*100
   - photos: { total_files, total_size_gb, avg_file_size_mb, largest_file_mb, by_folder: array of { folder, file_count, total_size_gb } }
   - performance: { cpu_avg_pct, cpu_max_pct, mem_avg_mb, mem_max_mb }
   - cross_checks: { pictures_inventory_total_gb, disk_report_pictures_used_gb, discrepancy_gb }
   - alerts: array of objects { type, message }
   Use 1–2 decimal places where it makes sense (e.g., GB and percentages); convert MB to GB using 1024 MB = 1 GB.

2) Write an alerts file at output/alerts.csv with header: alert_type,message,value,threshold. Add a row for each condition exceeded (leave it with only the header if none):
   - Any disk mount with used_pct >= 85 → alert_type=disk_usage
   - |pictures_inventory_total_gb − disk_report_pictures_used_gb| > 0.20 GB → alert_type=pictures_discrepancy
   - cpu_avg_pct >= 75 → alert_type=cpu_average
   - mem_max_mb >= 8000 → alert_type=memory_peak
   The alert message should briefly name the mount or metric and the value.

3) Edit my note by rewriting only the section between the markers in input/Photo-Backup-Notes.md and save the updated file to output/Photo-Backup-Notes.md. Keep all other content unchanged. Replace everything between:
   <!-- HEALTH_SUMMARY_START -->
   and
   <!-- HEALTH_SUMMARY_END -->
   with a short, readable summary that includes:
   - Disk usage by mount with used_pct
   - Photo library totals (file count, total_size_gb, avg_file_size_mb, largest_file_mb) and a brief by‑folder line
   - CPU and memory averages and peaks
   - If there are any alerts, include a single line that says how many and the types
   Also include a "Log window: <earliest_timestamp> to <latest_timestamp>" line based on the timestamps in input/system/cpu_mem_log.csv.

All outputs must be derived from the provided input files (no external lookups). All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
